title: Rule-family coverage ledger
purpose: Record which public self-verification rule families have executable positive, negative, and mutation coverage, without claiming full semantic coverage of every historical theorem family yet.
coverageScope: seed coverage ledger for the public self-verification stack; later PRs should expand each family until every corresponding checker and theorem binding is fully covered

ruleFamilies.title: Dynamic-programming induction and finite request/envelope reasoning
ruleFamilies.notes: The minimal kernel and shrink plan represent DPInd as a rule family and require future executable DP small-model coverage.

ruleFamilies.title: Global final-prefix and active final-node quarantine
ruleFamilies.notes: Current coverage checks that final theorem nodes remain quarantined and activeFinalNodeIds remains empty under seed fuzzing and mutation cases.

ruleFamilies.title: Locked NAND SAT-reduction checker surface
ruleFamilies.notes: The reduction surface is bound into the minimal kernel and trust base; exhaustive small locked-NAND SAT truth-table tests remain planned in TB-007.

ruleFamilies.title: Complexity implication SAT in P implies P equals NP
ruleFamilies.notes: The implication is represented as an explicit trust-base item and minimal-kernel spine family; a machine-readable complexity ledger remains planned.

ruleFamilies.title: Public theorem-emission gate and non-activation boundary
ruleFamilies.notes: The current seed stack repeatedly checks publicTheoremEmissionAllowed=false, finalTheoremReady=false, empty active final nodes, and exact remaining blockers.

ruleFamilies.title: Successor public-review report seal boundary
ruleFamilies.notes: Successor seal behavior is covered by shallow totality fuzzing and remains bound to the successor public-review boundary.

ruleFamilies.title: Explicit trust-base representation and shrink planning
ruleFamilies.notes: Trust-base explicitness, non-emptiness, checksum binding, represented-file checks, and shrink-plan non-activation are covered by seed audits and mutations.
